commands.review_summary:
The branch adds cooperative yielding and abort-aware error classification to Ollama native NDJSON stream parsing, plus dense-stream regression tests.

PR surface: Source +47, Tests +86. Total +133 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the scoped dense Ollama native-stream path: current main parses and processes dense NDJSON events without cooperative yielding, and the PR adds a focused regression test for timer progress. The broader live Windows local-daemon report was not rerun here.
commands.review_followup: - No automated repair is needed; this protected maintainer PR appears patch-correct and needs a human land or close decision for the scoped P1 mitigation.

target.title: fix(ollama): yield during dense stream processing
target.body:
## Summary

- rebase the Ollama dense stream cooperative-yield fix onto a current maintainer branch, preserving @udaymanish6 as commit author
- yield and check aborts while draining dense native Ollama NDJSON chunks so one large read cannot monopolize the Gateway event loop
- preserve caller cancellation semantics during dense stream processing and add regression coverage for timer progress plus abort classification

Refs https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599.
Supersedes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/86633 as the rebased maintainer branch.

## Verification

- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD` - passed.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts` - passed.
- `node_modules/.bin/oxlint extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts` - passed.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts --reporter=dot` - 1 file / 12 tests passed.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` - clean, no accepted/actionable findings; `overall: patch is correct (0.82)`.
- Blacksmith Testbox-through-Crabbox attempted but blocked by auth: `not authenticated — run 'blacksmith auth login' first`.
- AWS Crabbox changed gate: `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --target linux --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 180m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`; provider `aws`, lease `cbx_f9fbb521d6ec`, slug `amber-lobster`, run `run_2e1f46f56bc1`, machine `c7a.8xlarge`, exit `0`, `leaseStopped=true`, lanes `extensions`, `extensionTests`.
- Local Ollama live check was attempted but blocked: `ollama` CLI unavailable and `http://127.0.0.1:11434/api/tags` not reachable.
- After the AWS gate, `origin/main` advanced to `ee2b90b4e29864ba7456f6739228286f259eb60b`; the new base commits did not touch `extensions/ollama/src/stream.ts` or `extensions/ollama/src/stream.test.ts`.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: dense native Ollama stream chunks now cooperatively yield during parsing instead of draining an entire large read in one synchronous loop, while caller aborts during the drain surface as `aborted` rather than provider `error`.
Real environment tested: focused local Vitest wrapper in a Codex worktree, formatting/lint checks, branch autoreview, and AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: See the Verification section above.
Evidence after fix: focused stream tests passed with the dense-yield and abort regressions; AWS Crabbox `pnpm check:changed` passed the extension and extension test lanes on the refreshed branch.
Observed result after fix: dense stream unit coverage confirms timer progress during parser drain, aborts during dense processing keep `reason` and assistant `stopReason` set to `aborted`, and changed extension type/lint/import-cycle guards passed remotely.
What was not tested: true live Ollama runtime was not available in this local environment; Windows local-daemon reproduction from https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/86599 was not rerun in this PR. This is one mitigation in the broader event-loop starvation cluster, not the final issue closeout by itself.
